description:
You are working as an edge-computing engineer preparing a single-board IoT gateway for field deployment.  
    Management has asked you to gather a precise “pre-flight” diagnostic bundle that must be archived on the device before it leaves the lab.

    1.  A directory named /home/user/iot_deployment already exists and contains a file named devices.txt.  
        • Each line of devices.txt is a single IPv4 address that the gateway should be able to reach once installed.  
        • DO NOT modify devices.txt – only read from it.

    2.  Create a new sub-directory:  
        /home/user/iot_deployment/diagnostics  
        (Create it if it does not already exist.)

    3.  Inside that diagnostics directory generate TWO artefacts whose exact paths, filenames and formats must match the following specifications; **the automated tests will fail if there are spelling/formatting deviations.**

        a.  /home/user/iot_deployment/diagnostics/system_report.json  
            • UTF-8 encoded, one single JSON object (no surrounding array, no trailing commas, pretty-printed with 2-space indents).  
            • Mandatory key layout (every key must exist, even if some arrays end up empty):

              {
                "timestamp": "<ISO-8601 UTC timestamp, e.g. 2023-11-28T14:33:59Z>",
                "hostname": "<output of the hostname command>",
                "kernel": "<output of uname ‑srv>",
                "uptime_seconds": <integer seconds since boot>,
                "load_average": {
                  "1min": <float>,
                  "5min": <float>,
                  "15min": <float>
                },
                "memory": {
                  "total_MB": <int>,
                  "used_MB": <int>,
                  "free_MB": <int>
                },
                "disk_root": {
                  "size_GB": <float>,
                  "used_GB": <float>,
                  "available_GB": <float>,
                  "used_percent": <int>
                },
                "top_processes": [
                  {
                    "pid": <int>,
                    "user": "<string>",
                    "command": "<string, NO arguments – just the executable name>",
                    "cpu_percent": <float>,
                    "mem_percent": <float>
                  }
                  ... exactly 5 objects ordered by descending cpu_percent
                ],
                "open_ports": [ <int>, ... ordered ascending with no duplicates ],
                "reachable_devices": [ "<IP as string>", ... ],
                "unreachable_devices": [ "<IP as string>", ... ]
              }

            • open_ports must list every TCP port in LISTEN state on the local system.  
            • top_processes must list the top 5 processes by instantaneous CPU usage (use POSIX tools only; do not depend on htop).  
            • reachable_devices/unreachable_devices are decided by sending up to 3 ICMP echo requests (ping) to each IP from devices.txt; if at least one reply arrives the IP is “reachable”.  
            • All numeric values should be numbers, not strings.

        b.  /home/user/iot_deployment/diagnostics/ping_summary.csv  
            • UTF-8 text, Unix line endings.  
            • First line is a header **exactly**:  
              ip,status,latency_ms  
            • One subsequent line per IP in devices.txt in the same order as the file.  
            • status is either reachable or unreachable (lower-case).  
            • latency_ms is the average round-trip time across the successful replies rounded to 2 decimal places; use “N/A” (without quotes) if unreachable.  
            • Example (do not copy verbatim, your numbers will vary):  
              127.0.0.1,reachable,0.19  
              192.0.2.1,unreachable,N/A

    4.  When your script/commands finish, both artefacts must exist and be readable by the current user (no root privileges assumed).

    5.  For your own verification you may print console output, but the automated grader will only inspect the two files specified above.  
        Double-check spelling, file paths and JSON key order/capitalisation before concluding.  
        No additional files should be created inside /home/user/iot_deployment/diagnostics.  
        Good luck!
truth:
Initial filesystem state supplied to the container:

    1. Directory /home/user/iot_deployment/ exists.
    2. /home/user/iot_deployment/devices.txt contains exactly two lines (terminal newline after the last line):
       127.0.0.1
       192.0.2.1

    Automated checker expectations:

    • /home/user/iot_deployment/diagnostics/system_report.json exists and is valid JSON.
      – JSON schema must match the structure given in the task.  
      – hostname must equal the output of the container’s `hostname` command.  
      – reachable_devices must include "127.0.0.1" and must NOT include "192.0.2.1".  
      – unreachable_devices must include "192.0.2.1" and must NOT include "127.0.0.1".  
      – ping results may place additional IPs into unreachable_devices if devices.txt is edited incorrectly, but editing is disallowed and the test harness will enforce that devices.txt remains unchanged.

    • /home/user/iot_deployment/diagnostics/ping_summary.csv exists.  
      – First line equals: ip,status,latency_ms  
      – Second line must start with 127.0.0.1,reachable,  
      – Third line must equal: 192.0.2.1,unreachable,N/A  
      – No extra lines allowed.

    Permissions: both new files must be world-readable (mode 0644) so the non-privileged test harness can read them.

    The checker will also confirm that only the two specified artefacts were created inside /home/user/iot_deployment/diagnostics and that the directory path itself exists.
